name: Process Timeline
description: A numbered, narrative timeline that walks visitors through a process, methodology, or onboarding flow — vertical staircase by default, horizontal rail on desktop when the steps fit on a single line.

descriptions.descriptive: Sequential steps anchored by monospaced index numbers and a thin connector line. Each step balances a marker (week, phase), a verb-led title, and a two-sentence description, with optional duration tags rendered in tabular numerals on the right.
descriptions.usage: Use when the buying decision depends on understanding what working with you actually feels like — agency engagements, B2B onboarding, multi-stage products, certification paths. Place after the value-prop explanation and before the CTA.
descriptions.audienceFit: Best for B2B services, agencies, consultancies, healthcare procedures, and technical onboarding flows where evaluators need to picture the calendar before they commit. Reads as confident and structured rather than salesy.
